description:
This process diagram illustrates two sequential steps in spatial data processing, likely related to land cover classification at a 20 m resolution. The diagram shows transformations on a grid of 13 columns by 10 rows, where each cell represents a 20 m pixel.

The visible steps are:
2)  **Overlay of 20m grid**: Shows an irregular red-filled shape (resembling a 'C' with an internal white cell) and an irregular green-filled shape (to the right of the red one), both overlaid onto the 20m grid. These shapes appear to represent distinct geographical features. The red shape occupies cells from approximately column 1 to 10 and row 3 to 9. The green shape occupies cells from approximately column 8 to 13 and row 2 to 9.
3)  **Assignment of class codes at 20m according to defined aggregation rules**: This step depicts the result of applying aggregation rules to the previous overlay. The grid now shows a single, larger, continuous green-filled shape, which seems to combine the areas previously covered by both the red and green shapes from Step 2, and the internal white cell of the red shape is now filled in green. This indicates that the multiple input features have been unified and assigned a single class code (represented by green) at the 20m resolution based on specified rules.
